title: Throwing Errors 🚀
description: How to forcefully crash your own code when someone breaks the rules.

slides.title: Playing Defense 🛡️
slides.content:
# Enforcing the Law

Sometimes, you WANT your app to crash immediately so you can catch a bug.

Imagine you wrote a banking app with a function: `void withdraw(int amount)`.
What if another programmer accidentally writes `withdraw(-500)`?

If the code continues, it might ADD 500 dollars to their account! You must crash the code instantly.
We do this by **Throwing** an exception ourselves.

slides.title: The `throw` Keyword 🧨

quizData.question: What is the primary purpose of manually 'throwing' an Exception?

options.text: To immediately halt execution and alert the system that invalid or impossible data broke the fundamental rules of your code.
To log a small warning in the console without stopping the app.

quizData.explanation: Throwing is a critical defensive maneuver to prevent catastrophic logic bugs (like negative money withdrawals).

slides.title: The Custom Exception 🏷️

quizData.explanation: The 'throw' keyword physically launches the Exception object into the system.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: We can `throw` an error in one function, and easily `catch` it in another!
